pending.body: Transient rate limits during availability probes are still not retried. `AgentExecutionService::invoke` always calls `check_availability` before invoke (`src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:197-208`). OpenRouter's probe path returns non-exhaustion 429s as `AppError::BackendUnavailable { failure_class: None, details: "HTTP 429: ..." }` (`src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:214-231`), but `final_review_retry_failure_class` only retries `is_timeout_related` or `is_transient_codex_failure` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:221-227`), and that classifier's string list omits `429` / rate-limit terms (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:55-79`). So one of the motivating transient classes from the bead description, a transient rate limit, still fails a final-review reviewer/arbiter immediately if it happens in the probe rather than the invoke call.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 10 -> Plan and Implement
